issues.body: `role_of` returns `None` for both zero roles and multiple roles, so `resolve(["role:impl", "role:docs", ...])` produces a real default/security route instead of rejecting the malformed input. This contradicts the file's stated exact-match contract with `policy-resolve.resolve`, which raises `PolicyError` for multiple roles, and creates a default-allow path for any caller that invokes `resolve` without the planner's new precheck. The added self-test entrenches that unsafe divergence by expecting `sol`.
issues.fix_hint: Distinguish roleless input from ambiguous input in `resolve` and reject ambiguity (for example with a dedicated exception), matching claim-side policy behavior. Update the self-test to assert that multiple distinct `role:*` labels raise/reject; retain planner tests proving one malformed issue cannot cause an arbitrary role selection, adapting planner handling to the resolver's rejection as needed.
issues.title: Ambiguous roles fall through to a permissive default route

verdict.summary: The planner-side rejection is deterministic and its new tests are non-vacuous, but the shared route resolver now treats an ambiguous multi-role label set as roleless and sends it through security/default routing. That is not fail-closed and explicitly diverges from the claim-side policy resolver, which rejects the same input. Because route-resolve.py is independently executable/reusable and its contract says PLAN and CLAIM resolution must match, ambiguity must be rejected rather than converted into a valid default route.
